untranslatable_reason: EEDT (Exact Entry Double Top) is a candlestick pattern requiring visual/structural recognition of double top formation (two peaks at similar price levels with a valley between them). This geometric pattern detection cannot be expressed using only the allowed price-derived indicators (moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, etc.). Pattern confirmation requires swing high/low identification and peak-matching logic not available in the indicator set.
translation_notes: Attempted approximation: Entry assumes price rising above SMA_20 (uptrend before reversal) then crossing below pivot point (pattern completion/reversal signal). Exit on recross above SMA_20. However, this does not capture the specific double-top structure that defines EEDT. The 72% bear market success rate and 8% 5% failure rate from parameters should inform position sizing and stop placement if pattern is detected separately. Average decline targets: 25% (bear) / 18% (bull).
